news.summary: CEO Jamie Dimon says  JPMorgan  Chase’s stock isn’t cheap, but that hasn’t stopped the company from buying back $32 billion of stock over the past four quarters.  The bank has one of the larger share repurchase programs in the country, and uses more than half its annual earnings for stock buybacks. news.title: Jamie Dimon Sells $40 Million of JPMorgan Stock
news.summary: JPMorgan Chief Executive Jamie Dimon sold more than $40 million of the bank’s stock on Wednesday in the latest cash out of his shares.  Dimon has run the bank for over two decades and didn’t start selling any of his bank stocks until the end of 2023 when he sold a million shares worth around $180 million.  Dimon, his wife and his family trusts hold the shares.

signals.summary: Geopolitical crisis escalating: Iran closed Strait of Hormuz, U.S./Israeli strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure drove oil price surge, ceasefire collapsed, Trump announced naval blockade. Article suggests alternative oil investments may outperform Exxon.

affected_positions.rationale: Strait of Hormuz closure and supply disruption drive crude prices higher, directly benefiting integrated oil majors despite article's contrarian headline.
Geopolitical crisis escalation and Middle East military conflict historically drive safe-haven flows into gold.
Naval blockade and war risk typically increase demand for U.S. Treasury safe-haven assets despite duration exposure.
High-beta risk asset vulnerable to flight-to-quality during escalating military conflict and macro uncertainty.
Consumer discretionary faces headwinds from oil price shock raising input costs and squeezing consumer spending.
REITs face dual pressure from potential inflation spike (oil shock) and risk-off sentiment during geopolitical crisis.

source_event.title: Forget Exxon. The Smartest Oil Trade in 2026 Is the Company That Owns the Ground Beneath It
